_source: duplicated from data/processed/elo_current_ratings.csv — NOTE: this file is confirmed frozen at pre-tournament values, does not reflect real WC2026 in-tournament results (see project memory). Needs a point-in-time replay before use.

corrected_point_in_time_ratings._source: SUPERSEDED 2026-07-14 by ml/backtests/build_full_tournament_pit_elo.py -- see superseding_ratings below. This block (ml/backtests/fra_esp_sf_point_in_time_elo_replay.py, run 2026-07-13) held every opponent outside a small tracked set frozen at its pre-tournament baseline (a simplification used by every prior piecemeal replay script). For France that meant ALL SIX opponents (Senegal, Iraq, Norway, Sweden, Paraguay, Morocco) were frozen, vs only 2 of Spain's 6 -- so this number understates France's true rating more than Spain's. Kept for the audit trail, not for modeling.

France.path: Group: 3-1 Senegal, 3-0 Iraq, 4-1 Norway; R32: 3-0 Sweden; R16: 1-0 Paraguay; QF: 2-0 Morocco

Spain.path: Group: 0-0 Cape Verde, 4-0 Saudi Arabia, 1-0 Uruguay; R32: 3-0 Austria; R16: 1-0 Portugal; QF: 2-1 Belgium

diff_spain_minus_france.shift: -60.06 — stale ratings overstate Spain's edge; France's group-stage dominance (11 goals for, 3 against) isn't reflected in the frozen value

superseding_ratings._source: ml/backtests/build_full_tournament_pit_elo.py, run 2026-07-14. Systematic replay of ALL 100 WC2026 matches for ALL 48 teams -- every opponent's rating is itself genuinely point-in-time (no team held frozen), which is strictly more accurate than any of the piecemeal per-matchup scripts. This is now the canonical PIT Elo source for the project; data/processed/wc2026_pit_elo_panel.csv has the full match-by-match trail.

France.delta_vs_hand_built_script: +13.34 (opponent-freezing understated France more than Spain, see note above)

Spain.delta_vs_hand_built_script: -0.26 (negligible -- Spain's chain only had 2 frozen opponents)

superseding_ratings.diff_spain_minus_france: +22.84 (vs +36.44 from the hand-built scripts -- narrows the gap further)
